steps.action: Create the shared-types package (does not yet exist in the repo) with a package.json and barrel index so both FE and BE can import a single source of truth for priority contracts.

Define the priority contracts: a Priority value/type (mirroring the const-object + union pattern used in apps/taskapp/backend/src/tasks/task.model.ts), plus CreatePriorityRequest, UpdatePriorityRequest (partial of create), and PriorityResponse (id, name/label, numeric rank/level, createdAt UTC ISO-8601 string). Export from index.ts.
Create the in-memory Priority model/entity for the priorities resource (id, fields from the contract, createdAt as UTC ISO-8601), implementing the shared Priority contract from libs/shared-types.

Create CreatePriorityDto and UpdatePriorityDto with class-validator decorators (mirroring apps/taskapp/backend/src/tasks/dto/create-task.dto.ts); UpdatePriorityDto makes fields optional. These enforce 400 on invalid/missing input.

Implement PrioritiesService with an in-memory Map (pattern from apps/taskapp/backend/src/tasks/tasks.service.ts): create (randomUUID + createdAt ISO string), findAll, findOne (throws NotFoundException), update (merge valid fields, preserve unchanged, throw NotFoundException on missing), remove (throw NotFoundException on missing).

Implement the thin PrioritiesController (pattern from apps/taskapp/backend/src/tasks/tasks.controller.ts): POST / (201 create), GET / (list), GET /:id, PATCH /:id (complete the missing update path), DELETE /:id returning a success status. Delegate all logic to the service.
Create PrioritiesModule declaring the controller and service, then register it in AppModule imports alongside TasksModule.

Add Jest unit tests for PrioritiesService covering create, list, get, update (changed + preserved fields), delete, and NotFoundException on missing ids (pattern from apps/taskapp/backend/src/tasks/tasks.service.spec.ts).

Add e2e controller tests via supertest with a ValidationPipe-enabled app, asserting 201 create, 400 on invalid create/update, 200 list/get/update, 404 on missing id for GET/PATCH/DELETE, and that DELETE then GET returns 404.

Add a typed priorities API service that returns shared-types contracts for list, get, create, update, and delete; components must consume this rather than injecting HttpClient (pattern from apps/taskapp/frontend/src/app/core/api/tasks.service.ts).
Add a TzDatePipe to render UTC timestamps in the user's timezone (referenced by CLAUDE.md but not yet present in the repo), used by the priority views.

Build the standalone OnPush priorities list component using signal()/computed() for view state, loading via the API service and rendering fields plus createdAt through TzDatePipe; include a delete action.
Build the standalone OnPush create component with a typed reactive form whose validators mirror CreatePriorityDto, submitting via the API service.
Build the standalone OnPush edit component that loads an existing priority by id, prefills a typed reactive form, and submits updates via the API service preserving unchanged fields.

Introduce routing (the app currently has none): add an app.routes.ts with lazy loadComponent routes for list/create/edit, wire provideRouter into app.config.ts, and add a router-outlet plus a priorities nav entry in app.component.ts.

Add Testing Library component tests covering the CRUD acceptance criteria: rendering the list, creating with valid input, surfacing validation errors on invalid input, editing an existing priority, and deleting one.
